name: Northwest Plus
description_short: Northwest Plus is a credit union offering personal and business checking, savings, loans, mortgages, and credit cards with Kasasa rewards accounts and ITIN lending services.
description_long:
Northwest Plus Credit Union is a federally-chartered credit union serving the Pacific Northwest with a physical branch network and comprehensive digital banking services. The institution positions itself as a community-focused financial cooperative, emphasizing accessibility and inclusivity through Spanish-language support at all branches and ITIN lending programs for underserved populations.

The credit union offers a full suite of consumer and business financial products. Personal services include Kasasa rewards checking and savings accounts, regular savings, money market accounts, youth accounts, IRAs, and club accounts. Lending products span credit builder loans, vehicle loans, lines of credit, term loans, and extensive real estate options including mortgages, USDA loans, land loans, HELOCs, and home improvement loans. They also offer credit cards with promotional 0% introductory rates, debit cards, and merchant card services for businesses. Additional services include estate planning, TruStage insurance, and financial education resources.

Northwest Plus distinguishes itself through its commitment to financial inclusion and community impact. As a Juntos Avanzamos-accredited credit union, they explicitly serve ITIN holders and non-traditional borrowers. The Kasasa product line offers rewards-based checking with higher dividends, and they feature youth savings account matching promotions. The institution maintains transparency with published rate schedules, financial reports, and security disclosures, plus they host annual member meetings and scholarship programs.

The organization appears stable and member-focused, typical of credit union operations. However, potential members should note that specific eligibility requirements, membership fees, and exact competitive positioning relative to regional banks are not detailed on the website. The upcoming digital banking security update scheduled for April 2026 suggests ongoing investment in infrastructure. As a credit union, membership may require living or working in their service area or membership in an affiliated group. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Kasasa Cash Back and Cash Checking accounts with rewards
Regular savings, money market accounts, and youth savings accounts
Credit builder loans for credit establishment
Personal lines of credit (as low as 10.50% APR)
Mortgage loans including USDA loans and land loans
HELOC (Home Equity Line of Credit) and home improvement loans
ITIN accounts and ITIN lending services
Business checking (basic and premium) and business savings
Digital banking with mobile app and online portal
pros: Kasasa rewards checking accounts offer higher dividends and cash back on everyday banking
Credit builder loans explicitly designed to build credit while saving simultaneously
ITIN lending and Juntos Avanzamos accreditation serve underbanked immigrant populations
Spanish-language support at every branch location
Wide mortgage product range including USDA and land loans with competitive rates (6.288% APR on 20-year mortgages)
Youth savings account matching program (up to $25 deposit match in April)
Comprehensive estate planning services included for members
Online and mobile banking with bill pay, transfers, and CARDS+ debit card management
cons: Website does not specify membership eligibility requirements or how to join
No posted annual percentage rates for credit builder loans or personal credit cards (beyond 0% intro)
Limited information about specific membership fees or account minimums
Upcoming security update on April 21, 2026 requires phone number verification to avoid service interruption
No mention of fee waivers, direct deposit requirements, or qualification criteria for advertised rates
best_for: Immigrants and ITIN holders seeking credit building and traditional banking services
Customers wanting rewards-based checking with higher dividend rates than traditional banks
Families with youth members interested in matched savings accounts and financial education
Homebuyers seeking specialized mortgages like USDA or land loans with competitive rates
diagnosis: Northwest Plus Credit Union is best for community-oriented borrowers, particularly immigrants and ITIN holders, seeking full-service banking with rewards checking and specialized lending products. The main caveat is that membership eligibility is not clearly stated online, and many account terms and specific rates require direct inquiry or branch visit to confirm availability and qualification.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
